multimedia.alt: George R.R. Martin’s Favorite ‘Game of Thrones’ Spin-Off Gets a Colossal Season 2 Update
multimedia.caption: Aerion Targaryen in armor in A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms

fullArticle:
Earlier this year, the Game of Thrones universe gave us the best gift: a story that took us back to Westeros and closer to its smallfolk, leaving the court politics and dragon spectacle behind. A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms is a brilliant origin story of Ser Duncan the Tall (Peter Claffey) and his bald squire Egg (Dexter Sol Ansell), who’d go on to be King Aegon V and change the fate of the Targaryens.

The six-episode-long series showcased the events of Ashford Meadow, where the Knight and his squire come together, and the latter’s real identity is eventually revealed, followed by a tragedy. Based on George R.R. Martin’s Tales of Dunk and Egg, the show has been the most source-accurate, by far, and even healed long-time fans scarred by the finale of the original series.
